code: ### Suggesting Features  Feature requests are welcome! Please:  -   **Check existing issues** to see if it's already proposed -   **Describe the use case:**, why is this feature needed? -   **Provide examples** of how the feature would be used -   **Consider backward compatibility:** will this break existing code?  ## Development Workflow {#development-workflow}  We follow a Git Flow workflow with `main` and `develop` branches.  ### Branch Strategy  -   `main`: Stable releases only  -   `develop`: Integration branch for features  -   `feature/*`: New features  -   `patch/*`: Bug/hot fixes  -   `release/*`: Release preparation  ### Creating a Feature Branch  ``` r # Start from develop git checkout develop git pull origin develop  # Create a feature branch git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name

title: Contributing to BRCore

title: Introduction to BRCore
text: default settings identify_core() suitable analyses, function provides additional parameters balance computational efficiency ecological nuance. large datasets thousands ASV/OTUs, ranking every single taxon can computationally expensive. max_otus parameter allows cap number taxa processed. Rare taxa (low abundance) rarely contribute 2% Bray-Curtis similarity. Limiting ranking can significantly speed analysis without losing core signal. However, set low, may inadvertently exclude “satellite” taxa—low abundance highly persistent (high occupancy) across samples. default, identify_core() prioritizes occupancy (often taxon appears). However, may want account relative abundance (dominant taxon ). abundance_weight argument (numeric, 0 1) allows blend two metrics: abundance_weight = 0 (Default): Ranking based strictly occupancy composite metrics. abundance_weight = 1: Ranking based purely mean relative abundance. Intermediate Values: value 0.3 creates hybrid score, weighting occupancy 70% abundance 30%. Notably, incorporating abundance weighting likely result different core set occupancy-approach.

code: library(BRCore) library(phyloseq)  # Load example data data("bcse", package = "BRCore")  # Add rarefaction metrics bcse_metrics <- add_rarefaction_metrics(data = bcse)  # Perform multiple rarefaction bcse_rarefied_list <- multi_rarefy(   physeq_obj = bcse,   depth_level = 1000,   num_iter = 3,   set_seed = 7642 )  # Update phyloseq object with rarefied data bcse_rare_single <- update_otu_table(physeq_obj = bcse, rarefied_otus = bcse_rarefied_list, iteration = 2) # Your preffered iteration can be used here  # Identify core microbiome  # With a single iteration of rarefaction bcse_core <- identify_core(   physeq_obj = bcse_rare_single,   priority_var = "Crop",   increase_value = 0.02,   seed = 2134 )   # With multiple iterations of rarefaction bcse_core_multi <- identify_core(   physeq_obj = bcse,    rarefied_list = bcse_rarefied_list   priority_var = "Crop",   increase_value = 0.02,   depth_level = 1000,   num_iter = 10,   seed = 2134 )  # Visualize abundance-occupancy distribution plot_abundance_occupancy(core_result = bcse_core, core_set = "increase")  # Fit and plot neutral model bcse_neutral <- fit_neutral_model(   otu_table = bcse_core$otu_table,   core_set = bcse_core$increase_core,   abundance_occupancy = bcse_core$abundance_occupancy )  plot_neutral_model(bcse_neutral)

title: Identify Core Microbiome Using Bray-Curtis Similarity biological samples. Core taxa are selected using either a
text: physeq_obj phyloseq object least otu_table sample_data. phyloseq object already rarefied (.e., samples sequencing depth), can omit rarefied_list function automatically use OTU table physeq_obj single iteration. data rarefied, must provide rarefied_list generated multi_rarefy. Alternatively can use update_otu_table replace OTU table rarefied version running identify_core. rarefied_list list data frames, representing rarefied OTU table (taxa x samples) generated multi_rarefy. Required physeq_obj already rarefied. physeq_obj rarefied, can omitted function use OTU table physeq_obj single iteration. priority_var column name sample_data (e.g. sampling_date", "site") used prioritizing core microbiome. increase_value Increase value (numeric, scalar) used calculation (default 0.02) "increase". "elbow" always calculated returned elbow_core (see details). abundance_weight Numeric [0,1]; much weight mean relative abundance ranking score. 0 (default) uses occupancy/composite . 1 ranks purely abundance. Values blend two (e.g., abundance_weight = 0.3 gives 70% occupancy/composite + 30% abundance). max_otus Optional integer limit analysis top N ranked OTUs. NULL (default), OTUs analyzed. Useful large datasets (>5000 OTUs) depth_level Integer. sequencing depth used normalization Bray-Curtis calculations. data rarefied, automatically set rarefaction depth. unrarefied data, samples depth threshold excluded pairwise comparisons. seed Optional integer set RNG seed reproducibility.

(proportionBC), multiplicative IncreaseBC, elbow metric (elbow_slope_diffs). otu_ranked tibble ranked OTU/ASVs . abundance_occupancy tibble OTU/ASVs names, occupancy (otu_occ), mean relative abundance (otu_rel). priority_var character, variable used prioritizing core. elbow core set identified elbow method (integer). bc_increase core set identified last % BC-increase (integer). increase_value increase value (numeric, scalar) used calculation (e.g. 0.02). elbow_core core OTU/ASVs using elbow method (character vector). increase_core core OTU/ASVs using last % BC-increase method (character vector). otu_table otu_table counts (otu x samples) used (data.frame). sample_metadata samples metadata (data.frame). taxonomy_table taxonomy present (data.frame); otherwise NULL.

text: core set defined using two separate methods: function rank OTU/ASVs occupancy (optionally abundance weighting: rank_score = (1 - weight) * rank + weight * scaled_abundance, scaled_abundance mean relative abundance rescaled [0,1]). k = 1..K, recompute S_k mean Bray-Curtis similarity across sample pairs using first k ranked OTUs; k = K, yields S_K, value computed OTUs. Normalizing S_K gives C_k = S_k / S_K. elbow point diminishing returns: k, compare average left slope (S_k - S_1) / (k - 1) average right slope (S_K - S_k) / (K - k), choose k maximizes (left - right). last percent Bray-Curtis increase method uses accumulation curve, examine multiplicative step adding k-th OTU: Increase_k = S_k / S_{k-1} (equivalently, Increase_k = C_k / C_{k-1}). Choose largest k Increase_k >= 1 + p, p chosen percent threshold (increase_value; recommended p >= 0.02 2%). selects final rank adding one taxon still increases explained Bray-Curtis similarity least p.

title: Fit Sloan Neutral Community Model (SNCM) — sncm.fit
text: function fits Sloan Neutral Community Model community data assess neutral processes community assembly. compares neutral model binomial Poisson null models using various statistical measures.

title: Fit Sloan Neutral Community Model (SNCM) — sncm.fit
code: sncm.fit(spp, pool = NULL, stats = TRUE, taxon = NULL)

title: Fit Sloan Neutral Community Model (SNCM) — sncm.fit
text: spp matrix data frame rows represent communities (samples) columns represent species/taxa. Values abundances counts. pool Optional. matrix data frame representing source pool calculating relative abundances. NULL, source pool calculated spp matrix. Default NULL. stats Logical. TRUE, returns fit statistics including AIC, BIC, R-squared, RMSE model comparison. FALSE, returns predicted vs observed frequencies confidence intervals. Default TRUE. taxon Optional. data frame containing taxonomic information merge results stats=FALSE. row names matching species names. Default NULL.

title: Fit Sloan Neutral Community Model (SNCM) — sncm.fit
text: stats=TRUE, returns data frame model fit statistics including: m: Migration rate parameter NLS fit m.ci: Confidence interval m parameter m.mle: Migration rate maximum likelihood estimation maxLL, binoLL, poisLL: Log-likelihood values SNCM, binomial, Poisson models Rsqr, Rsqr.bino, Rsqr.pois: R-squared values model RMSE, RMSE.bino, RMSE.pois: Root mean squared error model AIC, BIC: Information criteria model selection N: Average number individuals per community Samples: Number samples/communities Richness: Number taxa analyzed Detect: Detection limit (1/N) stats=FALSE, returns data frame observed predicted frequencies along confidence intervals visualization.

title: Fit Sloan Neutral Community Model (SNCM) — sncm.fit
text: function implements Sloan Neutral Community Model predicts occurrence frequency taxa based relative abundance source pool migration parameter (m). model assumes neutral processes govern community assembly. Three models compared: SNCM (beta distribution), binomial null model, Poisson null model.

title: Fit Sloan Neutral Community Model (SNCM) — sncm.fit
text: Sloan, W.T. et al. (2006) Quantifying roles immigration chance shaping prokaryote community structure. Environmental Microbiology, 8, 732-740.

title: Fit Sloan Neutral Community Model (SNCM) — sncm.fit
